REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF ABDALLAH ELHANIA, ZAHRA ELMENJAOUI NOTICE OF MOTION TO DISQUALIFY DEFENSE LAW FIRM IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS A DISCOVERY MOTION MASKED AS A MOTION TO DISQUALIFY. UNDERLYING PLAINTIFF'S GROUNDS TO DISQUALIFY COUNSEL ARE ALLEGATIONS THAT DEFENSE COUNSEL ISSUED SUBPOENAS WITHOUT SERVING PLAINTIFF EITHER AT ALL OR IMPROPERLY, IN VIOLATION OF CCP SECTIONS 2025.240(A) AND 1987.5. PLAINTIFF FURTHER CLAIMS THAT THE RECORDS RECEIVED AS A RESULT OF THOSE SUBPOENAS ARE CONFIDENTIAL UNDER WELFARE & INSTITUTIONS CODE SECTION 827(A) AND CANNOT BE OBTAINED VIA A SUBPOENA. DEFENDANTS DENY ANY CLAIMS THEY VIOLATED THEIR DISCOVERY OBLIGATIONS. DEPARTMENT 501 IS NOT IN A POSITION TO RULE ON THE MOTION TO DISQUALIFY BASED ON ALLEGED VIOLATIONS OF THE DISCOVERY ACT UNLESS PLAINTIFF FIRST OBTAINS RULINGS ADJUDICATING THE UNDERLYING DISCOVERY DISPUTES IN THE PROPER DEPARTMENT. PLAINTIFF SHOULD FILE A MOTION UNDER THE CIVIL DISCOVERY ACT WITH DEPARTMENT 302 IN THE FIRST INSTANCE. BASED ON THAT OUTCOME AND IF APPROPRIATE, PLAINTIFF IS WELCOME TO RE-FILE THEIR MOTION TO DISQUALIFY IN DEPT. 501 AT THAT TIME. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). Off Calendar Mar-13-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ABDALLAH ELHANIA, ZAHRA ELMENJAOUI Notice Of Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel Airbnb, Inc. And Airbnb Payments, Inc.'S Further Responses And Compliance To Request For Production Of Documents Set Three And For Sanction.

Appearances:
Andrew Spielberger, Esq., by Zoom, of Balaban & Spielberger, LLP, (424) 832-7677, for Plaintiffs.
Warren Metlitzky, Esq., in person, of Conrad Metlitzky Kane LLP, (415) 343-7100, for Defendants.

The subject matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion to compel the AIRBNB defendants to provide further responses to the Requests for Production (Set 3) ("RFP") is GRANTED and DENIED IN PART.

The motion is GRANTED as to RFP 45, 46, 57, 58. The motion is GRANTED as to RFP 73, 74, 75, 76 but is limited to the Sunnyvale ordinance or Sunnyvale. The motion is GRANTED as to RFP 77, 78 to clarify that all documents have been provided (CCP 2031.220.) These requests pertain to Sunnyvale's short term rental ordinance and are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP Section 2017.010.) Defendants fail to show the requests are unduly vague or burdensome. Defendants must produce their documents, if any.

For the responses to which AIRBNB is claiming a privilege, AIRBNB shall produce a privilege log.

The motion is DENIED as to RFP 47-56, 59-72. The requests relate to cities, municipalities, etc. that have no relation to this case. The requests are not reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. The court further notes that many of the requests are facially overbroad and burdensome. (See, e.g., RFP 55.) An order is executed.

Visiting Judge: John True. Clerk: Robert Woods. Court Reporter: Carol Haraburda; CSR: 8052; T: 925.360.6065; E: chreporter@comcast.net.

Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Defense Law Firms
Off Calendar Aug-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs ABDALLAH ELHANIA, and ZAHRA ELMENJAOUI's Motion To Disqualify Defense Law Firms.

Plaintiffs' motion to disqualify defense law firms is recharacterized per the order filed August 6, 2025 as seeking a ruling that defendants violated one or more discovery rules and, as recharacterized, the motion is denied.

Plaintiffs have not shown that the subpoenas issued to the City of Sunnyvale or defense counsel's review of the documents produced the City of Sunnyvale contravened any provision of the Discovery Act or Welfare & Institutions Code 827(a), much less that defendants or their counsel willfully violated the Discovery Act or section 827(a). While there was a failure to provide actual timely service of the subpoenas on plaintiffs' counsel, that failure appears to be due to plaintiffs' counsel having different mailing and physical addresses and not due to any effort to make the subpoenas "secret" and unknown to plaintiffs. Nor have plaintiffs shown that they have standing to resist the disclosure of any of the records sought by or received by defendants from the subpoenas. Moreover, the records that were received by defendants were disclosed by presumably knowledgeable staff of the City of Sunnyvale who could have withheld disclosure if the records should not have been produced. Further, at least in part the disclosure of those records were later determined by the juvenile division of the Santa Clara Superior Court to be permissible. In all events, plaintiffs have not shown how they have been prejudiced by production of any of the records to defendants nor have they established any grounds for the extreme remedy of disqualifying defense counsel. This is a discovery order only and thus nothing in this ruling precludes plaintiffs from seeking, by way of motion in limine or in any other manner, an order from the trial judge precluding the admission of any of the records at trial.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)

Ms. Li and Ms. Taylor contests portions of the courts tentative ruling. Argued. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC. DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT

The Court notes that Defendant's notice of demurrer states: "Airbnb will, and hereby does, also move that if Plaintiffs' claims survive demurrer, they be stayed pending the arbitration of their claims." This request for relief is not supported by any argument or authority in the Points and Authorities and will not be considered by the Court.
The Court further notes that Defendant brings a brand new argument in reply (Reply 8:10-9:6), which will not be considered, plaintiff having no proper opportunity to respond and no explanation provided for only including the argument based on agreement to which the moving defendant was a party since 2018, i.e. for 6 years before the demurrer was filed. Further, request for judicial notice made in a footnote in reply with documents attached to the back of the reply is DENIED. CRC 3.1113(l).
Defendant's request for judicial notice of Terms of Service is GRANTED.
Demurrer to the cause of action for negligence is OVERRULED. Plaintiff states sufficient facts to support the existence of duty based on Sunnyvale Ordinance designed to ensure that short-term rental are not a nuisance or threaten the public health, safety, or welfare due to disorderly conduct; plusdefendant's involvement in the short-term rental beyond providing a platform for publication. (FAC 18-19). Whether this duty was breached and whether such breach was the cause/proximate cause of Plaintiff's damages is an issue of fact that cannot be addressed on a demurrer.
Demurrer to the cause of action for private nuisance is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts showing harm to a property interest.
Demurrer to the cause of action for public nuisance is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts (not conclusions) supporting each element of this cause of action.
Demurrer to the cause of action for violation of UCL is OVERRULED. Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that Sunnyvale ordinance does not apply to the moving defendant as a "person." Defendant does not cite to any definition of "a person" within the Ordinance or elsewhere that would exclude the defendant from the universe of "persons." Further, applicability of the Communications Decency Act is an issue of fact not resolvable on a demurrer. Allegations in the complaint must be taken as true and Plaintiff alleges affirmative acts undertaken by the Defendants (FAC 18-19) going beyond publishing third-party information.
Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the court's tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
